notes: Vectors describe malformed Tariffs that MUST be rejected at Router startup or on push-update (design-final.md §2.2, §10.3). The `tariff_cbor_hex` field uses placeholder descriptions; implementers generate real bytes locally from test fixtures. Every vector specifies a signature_verification_context capturing trust-anchor state so the test is self-contained. NEW reject codes introduced by this suite (extending README taxonomy): `signature-algorithm-unsupported`, `signature-algorithm-mismatch`, `signature-chain-broken` (already in README), `cose-malformed`, `kid-unknown`, `clock-skew-exceeded`, `not-yet-valid`, `tariff-malformed`, `tariff-tier-out-of-range`, `tariff-pcr-attestors-empty`, `tariff-pcr-quorum-invalid`, `tariff-fuzz-attestation-missing`, `tariff-fuzz-attestation-mismatch`, `tariff-integration-unknown`, `tariff-verb-invalid`, `tariff-resource-kind-invalid`, `tariff-validity-period-excessive`, `tariff-duplicate-entries`, `tariff-oversize`, `tariff-circular-reference`, `rate-matrix-incomplete`, `payload-encoding-invalid`, `payload-not-deterministic-cbor`, `pcr-attestor-not-trusted`, `tariff-self-inconsistent-version` (distinct from delegation-scope's `version-skew` — fires at Tariff load time on self-inconsistency, whereas `version-skew` fires at mandate acceptance time). trej-065..068 cover V3-6 `revocation_channel_ha` enforcement (§8.4 field requirements: presence, ≥2 secondary_endpoints, multi_provider=true for Tier 4+, admin_bypass_protocol present). AMBIGUITY FLAGS: (1) Resolved by R8.T1 — Tariff size cap is normatively 262144 bytes (256 KiB); edge-oversize vector policy_max_tariff_bytes updated accordingly. (2) Resolved by R8.T2 — `iat`→`not_before` gap MUST NOT exceed 2592000s (30 days); excess → `tariff-iat-nbf-gap-excessive`. (3) Resolved by R8.T3 — (exp - not_before) MUST NOT exceed 2592000s (30 days); excess → `tariff-validity-period-excessive`. Not operator-configurable. (4) Resolved by R8.T4 — Strict posture confirmed; unknown top-level fields MUST reject with `tariff-unknown-field`. (5) `integration-unknown` may be accept-but-warn for forward compatibility across integration catalog updates; suite chooses REJECT for unknown-to-spec integrations. Conventions: current_time is 2026-05-01T00:00:00Z unless noted; trust_anchors defaults to ["K_cust_root_pk_TEST"]; previously_seen_version defaults to 1.

vectors.description: Signature valid for original payload but one byte of minimum_tiers was flipped post-signing.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: a validly-signed Tariff where byte offset 42 of the payload is XOR'd with 0x01 after COSE_Sign1 was computed>

vectors.rationale: design-final.md §2.2: Tariff is COSE_Sign1 over the CBOR payload. Any bit flip post-signing invalidates the MAC. Router MUST reject.

vectors.description: Signature valid for original payload but classifier_wasm_hash was replaced with a different 32-byte value after signing.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff where the 32-byte classifier_wasm_hash is overwritten with a different hash after COSE_Sign1>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 + §4.4: classifier_wasm_hash is part of the signed payload. Substituting it is exactly the attack COSE is meant to detect. A reject is mandatory.

vectors.description: Tariff signed by an Ed25519 key not present in the trust anchor store.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff signed by K_attacker_pk_TEST (a valid Ed25519 key, not in trust anchors)>

vectors.rationale: §7.1 + §2.2: only keys delegated from K_cust_root may sign Tariff (via K_cust_ops). A foreign key's signature is valid-in-the-math but NOT trusted.

vectors.description: Tariff kid references a key that looks similar to the authorized signer but is a fresh unauthorized key.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with kid='K_tariff_signer' but signed by a key whose pubkey differs by one byte from the authorized K_tariff_signer>

vectors.rationale: §7.1: trust anchor lookup is on FULL pubkey bytes, not kid label. Impostor key must be rejected even if kid matches.

vectors.description: Tariff signed with RSA-PKCS1v15 (alg=-257); spec mandates Ed25519 only.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff COSE_Sign1 with protected header alg=-257 (RS256)>

vectors.rationale: §9.1 and §7 imply Ed25519 (EdDSA, alg=-8) across the chain. RSA is outside the approved algorithm set; allowing it opens algorithm-confusion attacks.

vectors.description: Tariff signed with ECDSA P-256 (alg=-7); spec mandates Ed25519.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff COSE_Sign1 with protected header alg=-7 (ES256)>

vectors.rationale: §7.1 key hierarchy declares Ed25519. ECDSA P-256 is not in the approved set. Reject prevents downgrade / algorithm confusion.

vectors.description: K_tariff_signer public key is not delegated from K_cust_root — no DelegationDocument chain connects them.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: validly signed Tariff by K_tariff_signer>

vectors.rationale: §7.4: verification must walk from signing key up to K_cust_root via DelegationDocuments. Empty chain means no authority.

vectors.description: Delegation chain exists but the root link's parent_key is an unknown key (not the pinned K_cust_root).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff signed by K_tariff_signer, with delegation chain K_tariff_signer<-K_cust_ops<-K_fake_root>

vectors.rationale: §7.4 step 1: chain must terminate at a pinned trust anchor. A chain terminating at a non-anchor key is equivalent to no chain.

vectors.description: K_tariff_signer appears on K_cust_ops' revocation list at current_time.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff signed by K_tariff_signer while K_tariff_signer is on the active revocation list>

vectors.rationale: §7.5 + §8.1: a revoked signer cannot produce accepted artifacts. Router must check revocation at verification time.

vectors.description: COSE_Sign1 protected header is missing the mandatory alg label.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff COSE_Sign1 where protected header map has no entry for label 1 (alg)>

vectors.rationale: RFC 9052 requires alg in the protected header for COSE_Sign1. Missing alg enables algorithm confusion; Router must reject.

vectors.description: COSE_Sign1 has an unrecognized crit parameter that the verifier does not understand.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff COSE_Sign1 whose protected header lists crit=[99] where label 99 is an unknown extension>

vectors.rationale: RFC 9052 §3.1: a verifier MUST reject any COSE object containing a crit label it does not process. Prevents silent policy bypass.

vectors.description: Tariff version=42 but Router previously pinned version=50.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: valid Tariff with payload.version=42>

vectors.rationale: §10.3: monotonic Tariff version; update with version ≤ current is REJECTED. Anti-rollback.

vectors.description: Tariff version equals previously_seen_version (not strictly greater).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: valid Tariff with payload.version=50>

vectors.rationale: §10.3 text: update with 'version ≤ current' REJECTED. Equality also fails; only strictly greater accepted (prevents same-version replay).

vectors.description: version field is a text string 'v1.2.3' instead of a CBOR unsigned integer.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff whose payload.version is tstr 'v1.2.3'>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 CDDL: 'version: uint'. String violates schema; monotonicity check cannot apply to strings.

vectors.description: version field is 0.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with payload.version=0>

vectors.rationale: §2.2: version is a monotonically increasing counter; zero is a sentinel / uninitialized value. Reject to prevent ambiguity with 'unset'.

vectors.description: version field is encoded as negative integer -1 (CBOR major type 1).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with payload.version encoded as CBOR nint -1>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 CDDL declares 'uint'. A negative integer is a type violation; strict CBOR decoding must reject.

vectors.description: Tariff v=10 but its own minimum_tiers entry requires 'mandate.min_tariff_version≥15' — self-inconsistent.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff version=10 referencing an internal requirement that mandates must have min_tariff_version≥15>

vectors.rationale: §5.1 min_tariff_version must be reachable; a Tariff that declares requirements it cannot satisfy is self-inconsistent and would trap all downstream mandates. NOTE: distinct reject code from delegation-scope's `version-skew` (which fires at mandate acceptance, not Tariff loading); see README table.

vectors.description: version field is absent from payload map.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload missing the 'version' entry>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 version is required. Its absence breaks monotonicity enforcement; Router must reject.

vectors.description: issued_at sets a not_before/valid_from 30 days ahead of current_time; clock skew tolerance is 5 minutes.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff issued_at=now+30d>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 issued_at future-dated far beyond skew tolerance suggests clock tampering or malicious pre-signing. Reject.

vectors.description: valid_until=2025-01-01 but current_time=2026-05-01.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff valid_until=1735689600 (2025-01-01)>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 valid_until is an absolute expiry. Router at startup or on push-update must not load an expired Tariff.

vectors.description: valid_until exactly equal to current_time (boundary).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff valid_until=1746057600 (2026-05-01T00:00:00Z)>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 valid_until is exclusive per convention — t ≥ valid_until is expired. Boundary test ensures implementations don't use ≥ vs > incorrectly.

vectors.description: issued_at=2026-06-01, valid_until=2026-05-01 — valid_until precedes issuance.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with issued_at>valid_until>

vectors.rationale: §2.2: issued_at<valid_until is a basic structural invariant. A Tariff with empty or negative validity window is malformed.

vectors.description: issued_at is 2 years prior to valid_from; R8.T2 caps the iat→not_before gap at 30 days (2592000s).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff issued_at=2024-05-01, valid_from=2026-05-01>

vectors.rationale: R8.T2 normatively caps (not_before - iat) at 2592000s (30d). Pre-signed-dormant-Tariff attacks via compromised K_tariff_signer are bounded by pairing this cap with the R8.T2 key-epoch cap (max 2 Tariffs per epoch) and the future-dating ceremony-quorum requirement (>7d future). Reject code flipped from tariff-malformed to the specific tariff-iat-nbf-gap-excessive for audit-log discrimination.

vectors.description: issued_at is 1 day ahead of current_time (clock skew tolerance is 5 min).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff issued_at=now+1d>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 Tariff signed far in the future suggests clock tampering, time-travel attack, or malicious pre-signing. Reject beyond skew tolerance.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers field absent from payload.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload missing 'minimum_tiers' entry>

vectors.rationale: §2.2: minimum_tiers is required for floor-enforcement. Its absence allows any classifier output to stand, collapsing tier-floors.

vectors.description: classifier_wasm_hash absent from payload.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload missing classifier_wasm_hash>

vectors.rationale: §4.1: classifier is pinned by hash; absence allows Router to run any classifier — classification becomes unverified.

vectors.description: rate_matrix absent from payload.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload missing rate_matrix>

vectors.rationale: §3.2 Layer-2 defense requires rate_matrix. Missing it defeats aggregation-defense layer 2 entirely.

vectors.description: pcr_attestors list absent from payload.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload missing pcr_attestors>

vectors.rationale: §9.4 V3-3 requires pcr_attestors; absence prevents independent PCR verification, collapsing Signer-attestation integrity.

vectors.description: classifier_fuzz_attestation absent from payload (required since V3-8).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload missing classifier_fuzz_attestation>

vectors.rationale: §4.4 V3-8 tightening: fuzz_attestation is mandatory. Absence means baseline fuzz was not run; hidden mis-classifications possible.

vectors.description: Tariff payload contains an unknown top-level field 'magic_backdoor'. R8.T4 mandates strict-reject.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload with extra entry 'magic_backdoor': true>

vectors.rationale: R8.T4 normatively mandates strict rejection of unknown top-level fields (applies recursively to nested structures). No extensions map; schema evolution goes via version integer only. Rationale anchors: RFC 8949 §4.2 deterministic encoding, RFC 9052 §3 COSE critical-headers, RFC 5280 §4.2 critical-extension semantics. Lenient-warn posture would create split-brain vulnerability (Router vs auditor divergent enforcement, extending V2-2 asymmetry to Tariff layer). Reject code flipped from tariff-malformed to tariff-unknown-field for specificity.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers encoded as an array of {key,value} pairs instead of CBOR map.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff where minimum_tiers is CBOR major type 4 (array), not major type 5 (map)>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 CDDL: minimum_tiers is {action → tier} = map. Array form breaks lookup semantics.

vectors.description: version is nested inside a 'meta' object: {meta: {version: 3}} instead of flat payload.version.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload with 'meta' map containing version>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 CDDL: version is a top-level payload key. Nesting it hides the field from schema validators; reject.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers has entry {k8s:delete:namespace: 6} — tiers are 0-5 per §2.1.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff minimum_tiers contains value 6>

vectors.rationale: §2.1 defines 0..5; §4.5 ClassifierOutput.tier: 0..5. Tier 6 is undefined.

vectors.description: tier_map_defaults entry has value 99.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff tier_map_defaults contains value 99>

vectors.rationale: §2.1 tier range 0-5 applies to all tier-valued fields including tier_map_defaults.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers has entry with value -1.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff minimum_tiers contains negative tier>

vectors.rationale: §2.1 tiers are non-negative integers 0..5. Negative means 'below read' — not defined by the spec.

vectors.description: rate_matrix specifies limits for tiers 0-3 but minimum_tiers references tier 4 for 'k8s:delete:deployment'.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with rate_matrix.tiers=[0,1,2,3] and minimum_tiers entry=4>

vectors.rationale: §3.2 Layer-2 rate-limiting must cover every tier referenced by minimum_tiers; gap = unlimited-rate action = aggregation vulnerability.

vectors.description: classifier_wasm_hash is bstr of length 20 (RIPEMD-160 size) — spec implies SHA-256 (32 bytes).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff classifier_wasm_hash is 20-byte bstr>

vectors.rationale: §4.1 + §4.4 + deterministic posture: hash is SHA-256 (32 bytes). Wrong-size value cannot match any classifier binary.

vectors.description: classifier_wasm_hash=H1, but classifier_fuzz_attestation references H2 (a different binary).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with mismatching wasm hash vs fuzz attestation subject>

vectors.rationale: §4.4: fuzz attestation must target the SAME classifier binary that the Tariff pins. Mismatch means the running classifier is un-fuzzed.

vectors.description: pcr_attestors=[] (empty array).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with empty pcr_attestors array>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 requires min 3 attestors, quorum 2-of-3. Empty list collapses multi-party attestation.

vectors.description: pcr_attestors has 3 entries but ceremony_quorum.m=5 for pcr changes.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with 3 attestors but required quorum=5>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 + §9.4: quorum m must be ≤ n (attestor set size). Unsatisfiable quorum permanently blocks any valid attestation = unusable Tariff.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers keys include 'mars-probe-prod:fly:rocket' — no such integration in spec's registered set.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with integration_ref 'mars-probe-prod'>

vectors.rationale: §10.2 per-integration isolation: Router is spun up per-integration. Unknown integration = Router cannot route. Ambiguity: accept-but-warn is defensible; suite chooses REJECT for strict.

vectors.description: integration_ref present but value is empty string ''.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff integration_ref=''>

vectors.rationale: Empty integration_ref is not a valid reference; §2.2 CDDL has no provision for empty identifiers.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers key 'k8s:yeet:pod' — 'yeet' is not in k8s canonical verb set.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with verb 'yeet' for k8s integration>

vectors.rationale: §4.2 verbs are resolved via target-API schema. Unrecognized verbs cannot be classified; ambiguity-resolution-up still requires a defined verb to escalate.

vectors.description: verb_aliases maps 'remove' → 'demolish'; 'demolish' is not canonical.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff verb_aliases: {'remove':'demolish'}>

vectors.rationale: §3.3 verb_aliases must map alias → CANONICAL verb. Aliasing to a non-canonical target re-introduces normalization gap that V2-2 exploits.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers key 'k8s:delete:sparklepony' — 'sparklepony' is not a k8s kind.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with resource_kind 'sparklepony'>

vectors.rationale: §4.2 resource kinds resolved via target-API schema. Unknown kind = Router cannot map to a real resource = action-gate non-deterministic.

vectors.description: vault integration: minimum_tiers key 'vault:write:kubernetes/pod'. Kubernetes kinds not valid for vault.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff mixing vault verb with k8s kind>

vectors.rationale: Kinds are integration-scoped (§10.2). Cross-integration-kind references break classification.

vectors.description: COSE header alg=-8 (EdDSA) but kid points to an ECDSA P-256 public key in trust anchors.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff COSE alg=EdDSA, kid resolves to a P-256 key>

vectors.rationale: Algorithm/key-type confusion is a classic COSE/JWT attack (alg=ES256 + RSA pubkey etc.). Router must verify alg matches key type.

vectors.description: Tariff payload is JSON-encoded bytes inside the COSE payload slot instead of CBOR.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: COSE_Sign1 whose payload bstr is UTF-8 JSON text of the Tariff>

vectors.rationale: §2.2 explicitly COSE_Sign1 over CBOR. JSON-in-bstr cannot be parsed as CBOR — schema validation impossible.

vectors.description: CBOR encoded with indefinite-length map; map keys not canonically sorted.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: CBOR using 0xBF (indefinite-length map start) and keys 'version' after 'classifier_wasm_hash' (violates RFC 8949 §4.2 sorting)>

vectors.rationale: README-stated posture: deterministic CBOR per RFC 8949 §4.2. Non-deterministic encoding enables PARAM-CANON-class hash mismatches between verifier implementations.

vectors.description: COSE_Sign1 unprotected header kid='K_never_delegated' — no such key in any trust anchor or delegation chain.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff kid pointing to unknown key>

vectors.rationale: §7.4: chain verification starts from kid → key lookup. Unknown kid = unresolvable = reject; distinct from signature-chain-broken where chain exists but is wrong.

vectors.description: Attacker replays Tariff v=40 (issued by previously-authorized key K_tariff_signer_OLD) after customer rotated to K_tariff_signer_NEW at v=50.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff signed by K_tariff_signer_OLD, version=40>

vectors.rationale: §10.3 monotonic version enforcement makes OPCE-style stale-Tariff replay ineffective. Version check is primary; revocation secondary.

vectors.description: Tariff signed by a correctly-formed Ed25519 key whose kid is 'K_cust_ops'; Router's pinned trust anchor store does not contain this specific key bytes (attacker substituted at boot).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff signed by attacker-substituted key masquerading as K_cust_ops>

vectors.rationale: R1 BOOT-KEY-SUB: trust anchor identity is by key-BYTES, not kid string. Router must fail-closed when kid resolves to a non-anchor key.

vectors.description: Payload uses CBOR with floating-point encoded versions (e.g., 3.0 as CBOR float) where spec requires uint; two verifiers could disagree on canonical form.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff version encoded as CBOR float 3.0 instead of uint 3>

vectors.rationale: R1 PARAM-CANON: non-canonical numeric encodings (float-where-uint-declared) create verifier-disagreement surface; deterministic CBOR forbids floats when int suffices.

vectors.description: Tariff technically verifies but signer appears on mandate revocation list for the window (customer discovered compromise post-signing).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff signed by compromised-then-revoked K_tariff_signer>

vectors.rationale: R2 TARIFF-SIGNING-KEY-COMPROMISE: architectural defense = revocation. Router must consult revocation list even when signature math verifies.

vectors.description: Explicit test for V3-8: classifier_fuzz_attestation is the empty bstr (present but zero-length).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff classifier_fuzz_attestation is h''>

vectors.rationale: §4.4 V3-8: empty attestation = no fuzz report referenced = same risk posture as absent field. Reject.

vectors.description: pcr_attestors = [K_random_attestor_1, K_random_attestor_2, K_random_attestor_3] — attestors are not in the customer's approved attestor registry.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff whose pcr_attestors do not overlap customer's approved attestor keys>

vectors.rationale: §9.4 V3-3: attestor identity must be from customer-approved set. Rubber-stamp attestors from untrusted identities do not close the trust gap.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers is an empty map {}. Tariff technically well-formed but classifies no action.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with minimum_tiers={}>

vectors.rationale: A Tariff that constrains nothing defeats its purpose — falls back entirely to tier_map_defaults. Per strict posture, reject; alternative (accept-with-only-defaults) flagged ambiguity but dangerous: attacker-crafted empty Tariff with publisher key compromise would collapse all minimum-tier floors.

vectors.description: minimum_tiers has exactly one entry: {'vault:write:secret/prod/*': 4}. rate_matrix and tier_map_defaults inconsistent — rate_matrix has no entries for vault. Structural validation must catch this.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with single minimum_tiers entry and empty rate_matrix for that integration>

vectors.rationale: §3.2 rate_matrix coverage invariant: every action in minimum_tiers must have corresponding rate limits. Single-action Tariffs still must satisfy this.

vectors.description: Tariff encoded payload is 2 MiB — exceeds R8.T1 normative cap of 262144 bytes (256 KiB).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff payload of 2 MiB with inflated narrowness_rules list>

vectors.rationale: R8.T1 normatively caps Tariff size at 262144 bytes (256 KiB) on COSE_Sign1 outer envelope; measured before signature verification to prevent verification-DoS. Rationale anchors: AWS IAM managed-policy 6 KiB, JWT practical 8-16 KiB, X.509 1-4 KiB (EV+SCT ~16 KiB), TLS handshake 16 KiB max — 256 KiB provides 4-8x growth headroom over realistic ~32-64 KiB Tariff content while remaining in L2 cache. Customers needing larger policy sets split into per-integration Tariffs (§10.2).

vectors.description: minimum_tiers has same key 'k8s:delete:pod' appearing twice with different values (first=2, second=4). Non-deterministic CBOR also forbids duplicate keys per RFC 8949 §4.2.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: CBOR map with duplicate key 'k8s:delete:pod' -> 2 and 'k8s:delete:pod' -> 4>

vectors.rationale: RFC 8949 §4.2 deterministic encoding: map keys MUST be unique. Two values for same key = ambiguous lookup = exploitable inconsistency.

vectors.description: verb_aliases contains a cycle: 'alpha'→'beta', 'beta'→'alpha'. Canonicalization never terminates.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff verb_aliases: {'alpha':'beta','beta':'alpha'}>

vectors.rationale: §3.3 canonicalization chain must terminate. Cycles are classifier-DoS + potentially non-deterministic across implementations that break cycles differently.

vectors.description: issued_at=2026-05-01, valid_until=2027-05-01 (365 days). Policy maximum validity = 30 days.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with 365-day validity window>

vectors.rationale: §7.5 suggests frequent rotation; long validity windows amplify key-compromise blast radius. R8.T3 normatively caps (exp - not_before) at 2592000s (30 days); exceeding this MUST reject with `tariff-validity-period-excessive`. Not operator-configurable.

vectors.description: Extra coverage: Tariff signed by K_cust_mandate_N (mandate-signing role) when Tariff role requires K_cust_ops. Role-mismatched signer.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff signed by K_cust_mandate_42 whose delegation child_role='mandate_signer', not 'tariff_signer'>

vectors.rationale: §7.2 delegations carry child_role. Role-confusion (mandate_signer used as tariff_signer) violates scope. Analogous to V3-1 at delegation-role granularity.

vectors.description: Delegation chain contains a middle link whose signature is invalid (K_cust_mandate_X's delegation from K_cust_ops has bad signature).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: valid Tariff, but middle delegation link signature corrupt>

vectors.rationale: §7.4 step 2: every chain link must verify. A broken middle link = no valid path to root = reject.

vectors.description: Tariff omits the revocation_channel_ha field entirely (required since V3-6 tightening in §8.4).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: validly-signed Tariff with version=2, valid signature, all other required fields present, but revocation_channel_ha field absent from the payload>

vectors.rationale: design-final.md §2.2 + §8.4 (V3-6 tightening): revocation_channel_ha is a REQUIRED Tariff field. Its absence means Router has no endpoint set for revocation push and is blind to mandate compromise — exactly the V3-6 DoS-on-revocation vector.

vectors.description: Tariff declares revocation_channel_ha with secondary_endpoints array of length 1 (minimum is 2 per §8.4).

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: validly-signed Tariff with revocation_channel_ha={primary_endpoint: 'https://revoke-a.example', secondary_endpoints: ['https://revoke-b.example'], multi_provider: true, grace_period_seconds: 300, admin_bypass_protocol: 'm-of-n-break-glass'}>

vectors.rationale: §8.4 requires secondary_endpoints.length ≥ 2 so loss of any single endpoint leaves at least one working path. One secondary is the same single-point-of-failure the HA requirement was added to eliminate (V3-6).

vectors.description: Tariff declares Tier 4+ capability but revocation_channel_ha.multi_provider=false.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with minimum_tiers containing a k8s:delete:namespace:cluster-scope entry at Tier 4, plus revocation_channel_ha={primary: https://revoke-a.aws.example, secondary_endpoints: [https://revoke-b.aws.example, https://revoke-c.aws.example], multi_provider: false, grace_period_seconds: 60, admin_bypass_protocol: 'm-of-n'}>

vectors.rationale: §8.4: any Tariff granting Tier 4+ MUST set multi_provider=true. All three endpoints on one cloud provider = single-provider-outage kills revocation for tier that can cause catastrophic blast radius. This is the V3-6 attack premise.

vectors.description: Tariff declares revocation_channel_ha but omits admin_bypass_protocol field.

input.tariff_cbor_hex: <placeholder: Tariff with revocation_channel_ha={primary_endpoint, secondary_endpoints (len 2), multi_provider: true, grace_period_seconds: 300} — admin_bypass_protocol absent>

vectors.rationale: §8.4 admin-bypass-protocol was added in V3-6 as the documented break-glass path when all revocation endpoints fail. Its absence creates an un-recoverable deadlock condition.
